message:
fix(doctor): resolve _is_bespoke_covered pattern matching bug causing false-negative warnings (#241)

The _is_bespoke_covered function stripped the trailing ".N" suffix from
manifest check IDs via _manifest_module_id (e.g. "stack.beads_rust.1" ->
"stack.beads_rust") but then matched the result against case patterns like
"stack.beads_rust.*" which require a dot after the base name.  Since the
stripped module_id has no trailing dot, these patterns never matched,
causing manifest supplemental checks to re-run checks already covered by
bespoke functions — producing duplicate WARN/FAIL results for tools that
were actually installed and passing.

Fix: match against the raw check ID instead of the stripped module_id,
and add bare module name alternatives (e.g. "stack.beads_rust|stack.beads_rust.*")
so both "stack.beads_rust" and "stack.beads_rust.1" are recognized.

This eliminates false-negative warnings for br, SSH keepalive, UBS, DCG,
CM, meta_skill, and all other multi-check manifest modules on clean installs.

Closes #241

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
